title: Dracontomelon lamujo
intro: Dracontomelon lamujo, also known as Lamuju or the Philippine Dragon Fruit, is a type of fruit-bearing tree that belongs to the family Anacardiaceae. The health benefits of Dracontomelon lamujo include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help in reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dracontomelon lamujo include teas, decoctions, and infusions, which are traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Dracontomelon lamujo is a species of tree in the family Sapindaceae. This species is part of the genus Dracontomelon, which belongs to the family Sapindaceae. The Sapindaceae family is a group of flowering plants that includes maples, horse chestnuts, and lychees. The genus Dracontomelon comprises various species of trees native to the tropical regions of Asia. The classification of Dracontomelon lamujo reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the Sapindaceae family.
